_comment: GAME-START relationship graph seed for all 9 Wang Family Village NPCs. Per Article XXXIV (relationships are graphs, not numbers — 'friendship > 50' is forbidden) and Article XXXV.3 (the Understanding dimension is NOT respect; relevance is universal, not player-special). Each entry is one NPC's graph toward one target. Values reflect PRE-EXISTING history before the player arrives: the elder's old jade-debt to the Wang family line (the Chapter 1 catalyst seed), the Wang/Zhou family dynamics, Wang Lin's absence at Heng Yue, the Teng family's regional oppression, and the Heng Yue recruiter's professional-mode assessment. The player is a stranger at game start — most player graphs are shallow (Art XXXIII.2: the player is not default relevant). DATA-ONLY (Article XXVI).
_note: Each entry is one NPC's graph toward one target. At game start, each NPC has graphs toward the other 8 village NPCs, toward key external figures (npc_wang_lin — absent at Heng Yue; npc_teng_huayuan — regional oppressor, not present), toward the player (mostly shallow/unknown — the player is a stranger), and toward factions (heng_yue_sect, teng_family, wang_family_village). Values reflect PRE-EXISTING history before the player arrives. The favor_debt_ledger_schema.json defines the structured favor records; the relationship_graph 'debt' dimension here is the aggregate scalar. Notable: npc_wang_tianshui (the village patriarch / Wang Lin's father) holds the strange jade catalyst in trust from an ancestral mandate — his debt toward npc_wang_lin (+7, Major) and toward the wang_family_village faction (+7, Major) is the SEED of the Chapter 1 catalyst. When the elder's trust toward a target (player or NPC) crosses the threshold, the jade is given and the debt is honored. The two Heng Yue outsiders (npc_heng_yue_recruiter, npc_zhang_tian) spawn on Day 3 for the aptitude test ceremony; their graphs represent the attitudes they arrive with.

graphs.interpretation: Tianshui's younger son. Trust 80 — raised him, knows his heart. Respect only 35: Wang Ping is gentle but a mortal with no special capability (canon: stays behind to care for parents while Wang Lin leaves). Debt +3 (Moderate): the elder holds the strange jade in trust for the Wang family line, and Wang Ping as the staying son embodies that obligation. Understanding 75 — a father knows his son.

Tianshui's wife, Wang Lin's mother (canon NPC file: relationship_to_wanglin=mother). Decades of marriage — trust 90, understanding 85. No structured debt between spouses. Relevance 90: she is central to his life. Her worry for their absent son is shared.

Wang Lin's childhood friend (canon NPC file: relationship_to_wanglin=childhood_friend). Tianshui has watched him grow up — a frequent visitor, simple and loyal. Trust 60, but respect only 25 (Da Niu is not wise). Relevance 25: noted as his son's friend and a useful pair of hands, not central.

Long-time neighbor; kind to the Wang family in hard times (canon NPC file). Trust 65. Debt -1 (Minor): Tianshui did carpentry for Zhou Rui ('He made my new shelves last week' — canon initiation line), so Zhou Rui owes Tianshui a small favor. Relevance 40: important neighbor, reciprocal help.

Wang family cousin (canon: calls Wang Lin 'Cousin Wang Lin'). A young family member Tianshui has watched grow up. Respect only 35 — no special capability yet, but her perception_tiers hint at latent cultivation. Relevance 30: noted, helps with ancestral hall.

Wang family elder, low-tier cultivator (canon NPC file: type=elder, cultivation=low-tier cultivator). Likely Tianshui's older brother or uncle — lifelong relationship, shared_history 30. Fear 5: even a low-tier cultivator is dangerous to a mortal. Relevance 60: Tianshan's counsel matters to Tianshui's decisions.

Heng Yue outer-sect functionary (Qi Condensation 3) dispatched annually for the aptitude test. Cautious respect (55) — a cultivator, even low-realm. Fear 30 — the sect could harm the village. Relevance 55: this recruiter holds the key to whether any Wang family child leaves the village. Article XXXIV: outsider dynamics, professional-mode.

Heng Yue outer sect elder, Foundation Establishment realm (canon NPC file). Far more powerful than the recruiter — fear 50. Respect 65: Foundation Establishment is overwhelming to mortals. Relevance 60: a senior sect elder commands more careful deference than the recruiter.

Wang Ping's father (canon NPC file: relationship_to_wanglin=father). Trust 90 — idolizes his father. Fear 5: Tianshui is stern. Debt 0: Wang Ping does not perceive the jade obligation (that debt is Tianshui's toward Wang Lin / the family line, not Wang Ping's perception). Understanding 70 — knows his father, but doesn't fully grasp his father's burdens.

Wang Ping's mother (canon NPC file: relationship_to_wanglin=mother). Trust 95 — closest bond in his life. Understanding 75. Relevance 80: central. Wang Ping worries for her frailty (canon: 'Mother could use the extra food').

Wang family elder, low-tier cultivator (canon NPC file). Wang Ping respects him as family elder but doesn't deeply understand cultivation or Tianshan's history. Fear 8: cultivators are intimidating to a mortal youth. Relevance 30: noted, respected.

Wang family cousin (canon: calls Wang Lin 'Cousin Wang Lin' — peer to Wang Ping). Grew up together, shared_history 18. Trust 70. Relevance 35: interesting peer, possible young affection left unsaid (mortal village social fabric).

Wang Lin's childhood friend (canon). Wang Ping grew up around him — Da Niu visited often to play with Wang Lin and now invites Wang Ping fishing (canon: 'Da Niu wants to go fishing at the south bend'). Trust 75 — guileless and loyal. Relevance 35: peer, fishing companion.

Kind neighbor (canon NPC file: 'kind neighbor; helps Wang family in hard times'). Wang Ping trusts him (65) — Zhou Rui offers thread and needles for mending (canon initiation line). Relevance 25: noted, helpful neighbor.

Village social fabric — peer friction. Da Niu is the strong simple farmhand, Zhou Rui the shrewd merchant. Trust 50 — mutual wariness. Debt -1 (Minor): Zhou Rui gave Da Niu thread for fishing line (canon task context), Da Niu hasn't repaid. Grievance 15: Da Niu resents Zhou Rui's slight condescension toward 'simple folk.' Mortal-scale rivalry.

Reciprocal graph of the above. Debt +1 (reciprocal: Zhou Rui did Da Niu a favor, Da Niu owes him — from Zhou Rui's ledger). Grievance 10: Zhou Rui finds Da Niu clumsy and slow. Numbers are consistent with the reverse graph (trust 50↔45, shared_history 10↔10). Article XXXIV: same relationship, two different graphs.

Wang family cousin (canon). Wang Qingyue has a soft spot for the gentle younger brother of her cousin Wang Lin — shared_history 18, trust 75. Relevance 40: interesting peer. Mortal-scale young affection, unspoken. (Compare with npc_wang_ping → npc_wang_qingyue: trust 70, shared_history 18 — consistent.)

Da Niu looks after his absent best friend's younger brother (canon: 'Da Niu wants to go fishing at the south bend' — invites Wang Ping). Trust 80 — guileless warmth. Understanding 65: knows him well. Relevance 30: noted, looks after him as Wang Lin's brother.

Elder's view of the family patriarch (likely Tianshui's older brother or uncle). Lifelong relationship, shared_history 30. Grievance 5: mild — Tianshan may feel Tianshui doesn't consult him enough on family matters, or is too soft on the Teng family. Relevance 55: the family head is significant to Tianshan's role.

CANON-CRITICAL. Tianshui's absent eldest son, now a cultivator at Heng Yue (canon: Wang Lin left the village to cultivate). Debt +7 (Major): Tianshui holds the strange jade catalyst in trust from an ancestral mandate — owed to Wang Lin as the eldest son. THIS IS THE SEED OF THE CHAPTER 1 CATALYST. When trust toward a worthy recipient crosses the threshold, the jade is given and the Major favor is honored (see favor_debt_ledger_schema). Understanding only 50: Tianshui knew his son's childhood self, but cultivation has changed Wang Lin — the father no longer fully understands the cultivator his son has become.

Brothers — Wang Ping idolizes his absent older brother (trust 95, respect 80). shared_history 22 (grew up together, then separated when Wang Lin left for Heng Yue). Grievance 8: mild, not resentment — Wang Ping grieves his brother's absence and worries about him. Understanding only 45: they grew apart when Wang Lin became a cultivator (Article XXXV.3 — you can love someone you no longer understand). Relevance 75: central, missed every day.

Mother to absent son (canon NPC file: relationship_to_wanglin=mother). Trust 95. Grievance 5: she grieves his absence and fears for his safety. Understanding 55 — better than the father's, a mother's intuition. Relevance 85: central, always on her mind (canon initiation line: 'Wang Lin, take this bread. You always forget to eat...').

Wang family elder's view of the Wang descendant who left for cultivation. Respect 75 — Tianshan, himself a low-tier cultivator, recognizes what Wang Lin has achieved. Fear 10: cultivation commands deference. Relevance 50: a point of family pride; Tianshan watches for news of him.

Childhood friends (canon NPC file: relationship_to_wanglin=childhood_friend). Trust 90. Grievance 10: Da Niu's best friend left him behind in the mortal village — mild sadness, not resentment. Understanding only 40: cultivation is beyond Da Niu's comprehension; Wang Lin is no longer the boy he played with. Relevance 50: Da Niu thinks of him often.

Cousin's admiration (canon: calls Wang Lin 'Cousin Wang Lin'). Trust 80, respect 70 — Wang Lin became a cultivator, a point of family pride. Understanding 45: she knew him as a child; cultivation has made him distant. Relevance 40: interesting, a figure of curiosity.

Professional regard. The recruiter (Qi Condensation 3) needs the village patriarch's cooperation to gather youth for the aptitude test (canon: 'gather_village_children' sect task). Trust 35 — knows Tianshui from prior annual visits as cooperative. Relevance 50: without Tianshui, the recruiter cannot do his job. Article XXXIV outsider dynamics.

Assessing cultivation potential. Hasn't met Wang Ping yet — trust 10, respect 8, understanding 5. Relevance 35 (interesting — assessing): Wang Ping is the right age for the aptitude test, but the recruiter expects low aptitude based on his gentle demeanor. known_skills empty — observation begins when the test happens.

Assessing — strong but simple, unlikely to have spiritual root. Relevance 25 (noted): the recruiter will test him but expects to move on quickly. Pure professional-mode assessment; no personal relationship.

Assessing. Wang Qingyue's perception_tiers hint at latent cultivation ('qi_condensation: Cultivator') — the recruiter may sense something. Relevance 28: noted, slight uptick in interest over the obvious mortals.

Recruiter's view of his senior — Zhang Tian, Foundation Establishment outer sect elder (canon NPC file). Fear 35: a Foundation Establishment cultivator is far above Qi Condensation 3. Debt +3 (Moderate): the senior elder mentored the recruiter; he owes Zhang Tian loyalty and dutiful service. Relevance 60: Zhang Tian oversees his work.

Reciprocal graph. Zhang Tian's view of his subordinate. Debt -3 (reciprocal: the recruiter owes him; from Zhang Tian's ledger, the target owes the NPC). Respect only 35 — a low-realm functionary, competent but unimpressive. Article XXXIV: same relationship, two different graphs (recruiter's fear 35 ↔ Zhang Tian's fear 0; recruiter's debt +3 ↔ Zhang Tian's debt -3).

Annual visits (canon NPC file: 'Annual recruiter. Appears at Wang Family Village on Day 3'). Trust 40 — cooperative but cautious. Zhang Tian respects the village patriarch as a useful mortal but is not impressed. Relevance 30: the patriarch is the gatekeeper to the youth Zhang Tian tests annually.

GAME START — player is a stranger (Article XXXIII.2: player is not default relevant). Wang Ping has not noticed the player; he is preoccupied with his family's hardship and his absent brother. Relevance 5 (irrelevant). Article XXXV.2: the player is one variable among many, not a special case.

GAME START — player is a stranger. Tianshui is busy with village matters, the Teng family's oppression, and the approaching aptitude test ceremony. Relevance 6 (irrelevant). Note: this is the elder who would later gift the strange jade — but only when trust crosses threshold (per relationship_graph_schema example: Trust >= 5 already enough for the catalyst, but only if shared_history and demonstrated worthiness also rise).
GAME START — stranger. Maternal wariness: a mortal mother protecting her family doesn't trust outsiders. Relevance 5 (irrelevant).

GAME START — stranger, but Da Niu is guileless and slightly more open than the others (canon personality: 'simple, strong, loyal'). Relevance 10 (top of irrelevant / low noted): he may approach with a fishing invitation if the player lingers (canon: invite_fishing_south_bend desire).
GAME START — stranger. Merchant's appraisal: is this a customer, a thief, or a waste of time? Trust 8 (wary — lower than the others). Relevance 8 (noted): assessing trade potential.
GAME START — stranger. Measured Wang family cousin, observes but does not engage. Relevance 6 (irrelevant).

GAME START — stranger. The family elder is wary of strangers (canon personality: 'formal, elder'; initiation line: 'Young one, respect your elders'). Trust 8 (lowest — elders are cautious). Relevance 5 (irrelevant).

GAME START — stranger, BUT the recruiter's job is to assess village youth. Relevance 30 (top of noted): the player is a candidate for the aptitude test, an interesting target. If the player is in the right age range, the recruiter will summon them for testing (canon: 'You. Come here. Let me test your spiritual root.'). Higher than other NPCs' player-relevance, per task spec.

GAME START — stranger. The Foundation Establishment outer elder is more reserved than the recruiter but also assessing — any unfamiliar face near a sect recruitment drive warrants attention. Relevance 20 (noted): observes but does not engage unless the player approaches.

Faction graph. The Heng Yue Sect took Wang Lin as a disciple — Tianshui respects the sect (65) for giving his son a chance at cultivation. But he doesn't fully trust cultivators (40) and fears their power (35). Grievance 5: slight — the sect is far away and didn't protect the village from the Teng family. Relevance 55: the sect holds Wang Lin's future.

Faction graph — the regional oppressor. Teng Huayuan (half-step Deity Transformation, canon NPC file) destroyed the Wang family in canon. Fear 75, grievance 65 — years of tax collection, squeezing the village to ruin (canon initiation lines: 'The Teng family's servants were in the market again, buying up grain'; 'We gave them half our rice'). Respect 50: Tianshui acknowledges their power even as he hates them. Relevance 75: central — the source of the Wang family's ruin.

Faction graph — the Wang family line itself. Debt +7 (Major): Tianshui holds the strange jade catalyst in trust from an ancestral mandate — owed to the Wang family line, to be given to the right Wang descendant (Wang Lin) when the time is right. THIS IS THE FACTION-LEVEL SEED OF THE CHAPTER 1 CATALYST (paired with the personal-level debt toward npc_wang_lin). Relevance 90: central — the Wang family is Tianshui's life's purpose.

Faction graph — the recruiter's loyalty to his own sect. Trust 80, respect 75 — the sect is his identity. Fear 30: the elders above him are far more powerful. Debt +3 (Moderate): the sect raised and trained him; he owes the sect his life and loyalty. Relevance 85: central — the sect is his life.
